description_long: This is a single, upright tulip flower with a slender green stem and elongated, lanceolate leaves. The flower head is cup-shaped, composed of several overlapping petals that are a gradient of pink to white, with the outer petals being darker and the inner ones lighter. The petals are smooth, slightly curved, and arranged symmetrically around the central axis, giving the flower a neat, radial appearance. The stem is straight and supports the flower head firmly, while the leaves emerge alternately along the lower portion of the stem. The overall structure is delicate and elegant, typical of tulips often found in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative roadside plantings. The coloration and form make it visually striking and easily recognizable as a tulip.
description: A pink tulip flower with a slender green stem and lanceolate leaves, featuring smooth, overlapping petals arranged in a cup shape. The flower is elegant and visually striking, typical for urban gardens or parks.
description_view_0: The tulip is seen slightly from the side, showing the curvature of the petals and the natural bend of the stem with two visible leaves.
description_view_1: A direct front view of the tulip, highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of petals and the straight alignment of the stem and leaves.
description_view_2: The tulip is viewed from another side, displaying the overlapping petals and the alternate positioning of the leaves along the stem.
description_view_3: A rear view of the tulip, showing the back of the petals and the way the leaves wrap partially around the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, overlapping at the base and forming a symmetrical cup shape typical of tulips. The arrangement is regular and evenly spaced, with each petal partially covering its neighbor.
